hero.subtitle: Japan Mongolia IT Consortium
hero.description: Connecting Japan and Mongolia through intellectual cooperation — fostering collaboration between Mongolian IT startups and Japanese companies.

mission.heading: Intellectual Cooperation
mission.body: The Japan-Mongolia IT Consortium connects Japan and Mongolia, fostering collaboration between Mongolian IT startups and Japanese companies — aiming to become a core organization in the IT industry and contribute to the growth of Mongolia's startup ecosystem.
mission.visionText: From trade to knowledge — evolving the Japan–Mongolia relationship into a knowledge-based collaboration.

whyNow.heading: Shared Needs, Shared Opportunities
japanBullets: Interest in digital innovation for mining, agriculture, and infrastructure — Mongolia offers unique testing environments
mongoliaBullets: IT as a strategic export for a landlocked economy

areas.title: Joint IT Projects and Programs
areas.description: Study and share the experience of IT companies from Ulaanbaatar and Shinagawa. Support joint projects and cross-border programs between enterprises and institutions.

areas.title: Outsourcing IT Projects
areas.description: Enable Japanese companies to collaborate with skilled Mongolian IT teams — combining technical expertise, reliability, and adaptability with strong PM and QA support.

areas.title: Co-Innovation Events
areas.description: Organize joint events with the City of Ulaanbaatar and Gotanda Valley Association to promote innovation and business collaboration between Japan and Mongolia.

areas.title: Digital Nomad Program
areas.description: Provide Japanese companies with access to skilled Mongolian engineers and professionals through remote and freelance team-building opportunities.

areas.title: Research & Development
areas.description: Develop cooperation in science, technology, and innovation. Promote academic exchange and training courses for designers and programmers.

areas.title: Startup Acceleration
areas.description: Support Mongolian startup entrepreneurs through mentorship, incubation, and knowledge exchange — fostering an innovation-driven culture.

stats.label: Japan–Mongolia IT projects across member companies
Mongolian startups engaged via EOR model (6 engineers)
Partner companies in Gotanda Valley network
ICT Seminar co-hosted in Tokyo with Mongolian government

milestones.event: MoU signed between Ulaanbaatar City and Shinagawa (Gotanda Valley)
Japan-Mongolia IT Consortium established as NGO in Mongolia
ICT Seminar 2025 in Tokyo — attended by Mongolian Foreign Minister

members.heading: Member Companies
members.description: Companies represented on the JMIT board — driving intellectual cooperation between Japan and Mongolia.

about.heading: Executive Director
about.bio: Executive Director at JMIT (2024~) and CEO of Tab Systems Mongolia LLC. Previously Engineering Manager at Rakuten Group, Inc. (2017–2024). Graduate of Tokyo University of Electro-Communications.
